Security: Add authorization check to claude.yml workflow (#906)

## Summary

This PR addresses a critical security vulnerability in the `claude.yml`
workflow that allowed any external user to trigger AI-powered actions
with write access to the repository.

## Problem

The `claude.yml` workflow could be triggered by **any user** (including
external contributors) by including `@claude` in:
- Issue comments
- PR review comments
- PR reviews
- Issue titles/bodies

The workflow has extensive permissions including:
- `contents: write` - Can modify repository code
- `pull-requests: write` - Can create/modify PRs
- `issues: write` - Can create/modify issues
- `actions: write` - Can trigger other workflows

## Solution

Added an authorization check step that:
1. ✅ **Verifies user authorization** before running any workflow steps
2. ✅ **Allows only**:
   - Organization members (`MEMBER`)
   - Organization owners (`OWNER`)
   - Repository collaborators (`COLLABORATOR`)
3. ✅ **Blocks all other users** (contributors, first-timers, etc.)
4. ✅ **Adds thumbs-down reaction** (👎) to unauthorized comments/issues
5. ✅ **Logs clear failure messages** for audit purposes
6. ✅ **Bypasses check** for manual `workflow_dispatch` triggers

## Changes

- Added `Check Authorization` step at the beginning of the workflow
- Step runs before checkout to prevent unnecessary actions
- Uses `gh api` to add reaction icons for user feedback
- Exits with error code 1 to fail the workflow for unauthorized users

## Testing

Manually tested authorization logic:
- ✅ MEMBER/OWNER/COLLABORATOR users pass authorization
- ✅ Other users fail with clear error message
- ✅ Unauthorized attempts get thumbs-down reaction

## Security Impact

This fix prevents:
- Unauthorized code modifications
- Unauthorized PR/issue creation
- Abuse of `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` credits
- Malicious prompt injection attacks
- Information extraction via AI prompts

---

**Security Severity:** Critical
**Impact:** Prevents external users from executing actions with write
access to the repository

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Phase 1 (Steps 1.1-1.6) — Authority Dashboard + Full Crypto Pipeline

Step 1.1: React admin dashboard with JWT auth + RBAC
  - Login page, Exam Dashboard, CreateExam, ExamDetail pages
  - Express backend with exam config API, paper/roster upload (multer, 50MB)
  - Exam parameters: exam_id, start_time, duration, n_stages, stage_interval

Step 1.2: Content encryption with root key K0
  - AES-GCM 256-bit, random nonce, WebCrypto API (node:crypto webcrypto)

Step 1.3: Forward counter-hash chain construction K1–K10
  - K_i = HMAC_SHA256(K_{i-1}, i) — irreversible forward-only chain

Step 1.4: Nested encrypted wrappers + Merkle tree
  - wrapper[i] = AES-GCM(K[i+1], K[i]) for controlled backward traversal
  - Merkle root = SHA256(leaf0 || … || leaf9), leaf_i = SHA256(wrapper_i)

Step 1.5: Device binding + ECDSA-SHA256 package signing
  - binding_key = PBKDF2(machine_fingerprint, exam_salt, 100000, SHA-256)
  - bound_kfinal = AES-GCM(binding_key, K10) per student
  - ECDSA P-256 signature over entire package payload

Step 1.6: Distribution enablement
  - One-time download links (24-hour expiry, single-use)
  - Audit logs: exam creation, paper upload, roster, generation, distribution, downloads
  - Package hash and Merkle root stored in audit trail

Tech stack: Node.js + Express + lowdb (JSON), React + Vite, JWT

commit.message:
feat: add H146-H148 security heuristics (SBO, NPD, memcpy bounds)

H146: Stack Buffer Overflow GetValues — detects numeric array tags
where GetSize() exceeds kMaxSafeChannels (16), preventing stack
buffer overflow when GetValues() writes into fixed-size buffers.
CWE-121. Covers PoCs #551, #618, #649, #625, #624, #537.

H147: Null Pointer After Tag Read — validates post-Read() state
of tags that leave internal pointers null on malformed data
(Utf16Text, TextDescription, MPE elements, tag table entries).
CWE-476. Covers PoCs #553, #560, #484, #485, #507, #633.

H148: Memory Copy Bounds Overlap — analyzes MPE element chains
for channel count oscillation causing memcpy src/dst overlap in
Apply() ping-pong buffers. Validates NamedColor2 deviceCoords <= 15.
CWE-119. Covers PoC #577.

Heuristic count: 145 → 148. uniqueCVEs: 85 → 87.
All 217 tests pass. 0 ASAN/UBSAN errors on diverse profiles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct UV normalisation condition in phi_ubt_derivation.tex §1(c) (#381)

* Initial plan

* fix: correct UV normalisation in subsection 1(c) of phi_ubt_derivation.tex

The displayed equation in subsection 1(c) read "1 = 3/(2πR_ψ²) + 1/π²"
with text claiming "α = 1/(4π)" normalization. However, the formula
R_ψ^SC = √(3π/(8π³−2)) ≈ 0.196 that follows is correct only for the
"4π = ..." equation (i.e. α = 1 normalization). Fix the text and
displayed equation to consistently state α = 1 (strong coupling at the
UBT cutoff scale, bracket = 4π), matching the problem statement spec
"Φ × C5 × N_eff + spectral = 4π".

The Blender Foundation no longer hosts a standalone 720p MP4; all

bbb_sunflower_1080p_30fps_normal.mp4.zip (~275 MiB) and add a zip

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix patch_size for pixel-space training (2 → 16)

In latent space, patch_size=2 on 128×128 inputs gave 4,096 tokens.
After switching to pixel-space (1024×1024), that same patch_size=2
produced 262,144 tokens — a 64× increase that caused ~10× wall-clock
slowdown (O(n²) attention).

patch_size=16 in pixel space covers the same 16×16 real-pixel area
as patch_size=2 did in 8× downsampled VAE latent space, restoring
the original 4,096 token sequence length at full resolution.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: type coercion, closure scoping, class inheritance conformance (#450)

* fix: use string_to_number in abstract_eq for spec-compliant String→Number coercion

The abstract_eq function in the interpreter used Rust's f64::parse() for
String→Number coercion, which does not follow ECMAScript semantics:
- Empty string "" parsed to NaN instead of 0
- Hex/octal/binary literals ("0x1A", "0o17", "0b101") were not handled

This caused incorrect results for:
- "" == false  (should be true: "" → 0, false → 0)
- [] == false  (should be true: [] → "" → 0, false → 0)
- [] == ![]    (should be true: ![] → false, then same as above)

Fix: make string_to_number pub(crate) and use it in abstract_eq instead of
the raw Rust parse, matching the is_loosely_equal_inner implementation in
value.rs which already used string_to_number correctly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix closure variable capture through scope chain

- Add closure_context field to BytecodeArray to store the captured
  enclosing context when CreateClosure creates a function closure.

- CreateClosure now captures the current frame context so inner
  functions can walk the scope chain to reach outer variables.

- All call dispatch sites (CallAnyReceiver, CallUndefinedReceiver0/1/2,
  CallProperty, CallWithSpread, Construct, ConstructWithSpread,
  ConstructForwardAllArgs, eval fallback, dispatch_call,
  dispatch_call_property) restore the closure context on the callee
  frame via restore_closure_context().

- TailCall handler restores closure context instead of setting it to
  None.

- Generator run_generator_step restores closure context so generator
  closures can access outer scope variables.

- LdaLookupContextSlot and LdaLookupContextSlotInsideTypeof now use
  the slot_idx and depth operands to read from the context chain (fast
  path) before falling back to global name-based lookup (slow path).
  Previously they ignored slot/depth and only did global lookup.

- Remove incorrect callee_frame.context = Some(this_val) assignments
  in CallProperty / Construct / ConstructWithSpread handlers that
  were overwriting the scope chain context with the receiver object.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix class inheritance conformance: construction, super(), new.target, fields

- handle_construct (Function): Bind 'this' in global env so constructor
  body can access it via LdaGlobal.
- handle_construct (PlainObject): Handle JsValue::Function in __call__
  for class constructors created by CreateClass. Create this object,
  wire prototype chain, set new_target, run field initializer.
- handle_construct_with_spread: Same PlainObject fix.
- handle_construct_forward_all_args: Add proper [[Construct]] semantics
  (create this, set new_target, handle PlainObject class ctors).
- handle_call_any_receiver (PlainObject): Handle Function in __call__
  so super() works in derived constructors.
- Parser: Handle TokenKind::Super as identifier 'super' in primary
  expressions so super() and super.method() parse correctly.
- Add call_plain_object_function helper for super() calls.
- Add construct_class_from_plain_object shared helper.
- Add 7 end-to-end class conformance tests covering: construction with
  this binding, static methods, static fields, instance fields,
  extends prototype chain, super() in constructors, new.target.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Align "Unhealthy" stat card color in Fleet to error.main (#2211)

`FleetOverviewTab` rendered its "Unhealthy" stat card in `warning.main`
(yellow), while `InventoryHealthSummaryCards` (Indices/Data Streams)
used `error.main` (red) for the same label — inconsistent severity
signal across pages.

- Changed `FleetOverviewTab.tsx` "Unhealthy" `FleetStatCard` color from
`"warning.main"` to `"error.main"`

```diff
 <FleetStatCard
   title="Unhealthy"
   value={serverStatus.unhealthy}
-  color="warning.main"
+  color="error.main"
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>[ux-design-patrol] InventoryHealthSummaryCards maps
"Unhealthy" to error.main (red) while Fleet maps it to warning.main
(yellow)</issue_title>
> <issue_description>## Finding
> 
> Commit `d41133f2` introduced `InventoryHealthSummaryCards.tsx`, shared
by the Indices and Data Streams pages. Its "Unhealthy" stat card renders
the count in `error.main` (red). The pre-existing Fleet overview
(`FleetOverviewTab.tsx`) renders its "Unhealthy" stat card in
`warning.main` (yellow/orange). Users who switch between pages see the
same word "Unhealthy" convey two different severity signals through
color alone.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Evidence
> 
> **New pattern — `peek/src/components/InventoryHealthSummaryCards.tsx`
line 94** (commit `d41133f2`)
> ```tsx
> color: unhealthy > 0 ? "error.main" : "text.primary",
> ```
> 
> **Established pattern —
`peek/src/components/fleet/FleetOverviewTab.tsx` line 42**
> ```tsx
> color="warning.main"
> ```
> 
> ---
> 
> ## User impact
> 
> A user checking cluster health across pages will see "Unhealthy"
indices in red but "Unhealthy" Fleet agents in yellow — implying
different severity levels for the same concept, undermining the
color-as-severity language of the design system.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Suggested fix
> 
> Align on a single token for the "Unhealthy" status across both
surfaces. The Elasticsearch health model treats "red/unhealthy" as the
most severe state, so `error.main` is semantically correct.
`FleetOverviewTab.tsx` should be updated to use `color="error.main"` for
its "Unhealthy" card to match, unless the product team intentionally
treats fleet-agent unhealthy as a lower severity (in which case
`InventoryHealthSummaryCards` should downgrade to `warning.main`).
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Not a duplicate
> 
> Previous findings (#884, elastic/ai-github-actions-playground#1744)
addressed Fleet staleness chip color and agent table status taxonomy
respectively — neither covers the cross-page mismatch between Fleet
overview stat cards and Indices/Data Streams stat cards for the same
"Unhealthy" label introduced by this new shared component.
>
